description: Persist PR data at poll time and surface cycle time metrics: time-to-first-review, total cycle time (first commit → merge), PR size (lines changed), iteration count

userStories.title: DB migration: pr_snapshots table
userStories.description: Add migration in db.rs open_db() migrations array to create pr_snapshots table. This table stores one row per (repo_path, pr_number) updated on each poll — overwritten via INSERT OR REPLACE. Stores all raw fields needed to compute cycle time metrics at query time.
acceptanceCriteria: Migration added as a new M::up() entry in the migrations vec — never edits existing migrations

Table: pr_snapshots(id, repo_path TEXT NOT NULL, pr_number INTEGER NOT NULL, title TEXT NOT NULL, url TEXT NOT NULL, state TEXT NOT NULL, head_ref TEXT NOT NULL, base_ref TEXT NOT NULL, author_login TEXT, created_at_gh TEXT, merged_at TEXT, closed_at TEXT, first_review_at TEXT, additions INTEGER, deletions INTEGER, commits INTEGER, iteration_count INTEGER, updated_at TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now')))
UNIQUE INDEX on (repo_path, pr_number)
Index on (repo_path, state)
open_db() still returns Ok with existing DBs (migration is additive)

userStories.title: gh CLI: fetch PR detail with metrics fields
userStories.description: Add fn fetch_pr_details(repo_path: &str) -> Option<Vec<GhPrDetail>> in enrichment.rs. Uses `gh pr list` with expanded JSON fields: number, title, url, state, headRefName, baseRefName, author, createdAt, mergedAt, closedAt, reviews, additions, deletions, commits. Returns None on any failure.
acceptanceCriteria: Calls `gh pr list --state all --limit 50 --json number,title,url,state,headRefName,baseRefName,author,createdAt,mergedAt,closedAt,reviews,additions,deletions,commits`
Runs from repo_path directory (same pattern as fetch_prs / fetch_reviewed_prs)
Thread + handle.join() timeout pattern (no recv_timeout — same as existing pattern)
Returns None silently on any failure: gh unavailable, not authenticated, not a GitHub repo, non-zero exit, parse error
GhPrDetail struct derives Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize
GhPrDetail.author is Option<GhReviewAuthor> (reuses existing type)
GhPrDetail.reviews is Vec<GhReview> (reuses existing type) — used to extract first_review_at
GhPrDetail.commits is Vec<GhCommit> where GhCommit { oid: String, committed_date: String } — used for oldest commit timestamp

userStories.title: DB: upsert_pr_snapshot insert function
userStories.description: Add pub fn upsert_pr_snapshot(conn, ...) -> anyhow::Result<()> in db.rs. Derives first_review_at from reviews vec (earliest non-PENDING review). Uses INSERT OR REPLACE semantics via the unique index on (repo_path, pr_number).
acceptanceCriteria: fn upsert_pr_snapshot(conn: &Connection, repo_path: &str, pr: &GhPrDetail) -> anyhow::Result<()>
Computes first_review_at = min(review.submitted_at) across all reviews where state != 'PENDING', or None if no reviews
Computes iteration_count = number of distinct CHANGES_REQUESTED reviews
Uses INSERT OR REPLACE INTO pr_snapshots (...) VALUES (...) — replaces entire row on conflict
Returns Ok(()) on success, Err on DB error
Does not panic if reviews or commits vec is empty

userStories.title: Poll-time PR collection: collect_pr_snapshots()
userStories.description: Add pub fn collect_pr_snapshots(repo_paths: &[PathBuf], conn: &Connection) in enrichment.rs. Mirrors collect_reviews() structure: dedup worktrees to main repo, fetch PR details once per main repo, upsert all returned PRs.
acceptanceCriteria: Silently returns if gh not available (gh_available() check)
Deduplicates worktrees to main repo using repo_scanner::is_worktree + resolve_main_repo (same as collect_reviews)
Calls fetch_pr_details() per main repo; skips on None
Calls db::upsert_pr_snapshot() for each returned PR
Logs at debug level: 'Upserted PR #{} for {}' on success, warn on DB error
Only stores PRs for which the current user is author OR for any PR (store all — filtering happens at query time)
fetch_pr_details uses --state all so merged/closed PRs are included

userStories.title: Integrate collect_pr_snapshots into full_scan()
userStories.description: Call enrichment::collect_pr_snapshots(&repos, conn) in poller.rs full_scan() after collect_reviews(). PR snapshots are now persisted on every full scan (every 30 min) rather than fetched live at query time.
acceptanceCriteria: collect_pr_snapshots called in full_scan() after collect_reviews() line
Signature: collect_pr_snapshots(repos: &[PathBuf], conn: &Connection)
cargo build passes
collect_reviews() call is unchanged

userStories.title: Query: PrCycleStats and query_pr_cycle_stats()
userStories.description: Add PrCycleStats struct and query_pr_cycle_stats(conn, repo_path_filter, from, to) in query.rs. Reads from pr_snapshots table, computes per-PR metrics, returns aggregated stats.
acceptanceCriteria: PrCycleStats { prs: Vec<PrMetrics>, median_cycle_time_hours: Option<f64>, median_time_to_first_review_hours: Option<f64>, median_pr_size_lines: Option<f64>, median_iteration_count: Option<f64>, total_prs: usize, merged_prs: usize }

PrMetrics { pr_number: i64, title: String, url: String, state: String, cycle_time_hours: Option<f64>, time_to_first_review_hours: Option<f64>, size_lines: Option<i64>, iteration_count: Option<i64>, created_at: Option<DateTime<Utc>>, merged_at: Option<DateTime<Utc>> }
cycle_time_hours = (merged_at - created_at_gh).num_seconds() / 3600.0, None if not merged
time_to_first_review_hours = (first_review_at - created_at_gh).num_seconds() / 3600.0, None if no reviews
size_lines = additions + deletions, None if both NULL
Filter: created_at_gh >= from AND created_at_gh <= to
repo_path_filter: Option<&str> — None = all repos
Medians computed only over non-None values; None if 0 values
Empty table returns PrCycleStats with all None medians and 0 counts

userStories.title: Output: render_pr_cycle_stats()
userStories.description: Add render_pr_cycle_stats(stats: &PrCycleStats) -> String in output.rs. Pretty-prints cycle time metrics section. Follows existing render_summary style: colored headers, per-PR table rows.
acceptanceCriteria: Header: '=== PR Cycle Time ===' (bold cyan)
Summary line: 'N PRs opened  M merged'
If 0 PRs: 'No PR data available for this period'
Median row: 'Cycle time (median):          Xh Ym' or 'n/a' if None
Median row: 'Time to first review (median): Xh Ym' or 'n/a'
Median row: 'PR size (median):              N lines' or 'n/a'
Median row: 'Iteration count (median):      N.N' or 'n/a'
Per-PR table: PR# | title (truncated 40 chars) | state | cycle | size
Merged PRs shown first, then open, then closed-without-merge
No scoring language — cycle time is a measurement not an evaluation

userStories.title: CLI command: blackbox prs
userStories.description: Add Prs variant to Commands enum in cli.rs and dispatch arm in main.rs. Accepts --days N (default 30), --repo filter, and --format pretty|json flags. Queries pr_snapshots and renders cycle time metrics.
acceptanceCriteria: blackbox prs runs without error
blackbox prs --days 7 shows last 7 days
blackbox prs --repo <path> filters to one repo
blackbox prs --format json emits valid JSON
blackbox prs --days 0 returns error: '--days must be >= 1'
Default: 30 days, all repos, pretty format
Not exempt from config check (requires DB)

userStories.title: JSON output for pr cycle stats
userStories.description: Add JsonPrCycleStats and JsonPrMetrics structs in output.rs. Add render_pr_cycle_json(stats: &PrCycleStats) -> String. Ensure all fields serializable.
acceptanceCriteria: JsonPrCycleStats { total_prs, merged_prs, median_cycle_time_hours, median_time_to_first_review_hours, median_pr_size_lines, median_iteration_count, prs: Vec<JsonPrMetrics> }
JsonPrMetrics matches PrMetrics fields with Option<f64>/Option<i64> serialized as null when None
#[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")] on optional fields
render_pr_cycle_json returns valid JSON string via serde_json::to_string_pretty
DateTime fields serialized as RFC3339 strings

userStories.title: Integration test: DB migration and upsert
userStories.description: In tests/pr_cycle_test.rs, verify migration runs cleanly on a fresh DB and upsert_pr_snapshot inserts and updates correctly.
acceptanceCriteria: open_db() on fresh tempfile succeeds and pr_snapshots table exists
upsert_pr_snapshot inserts a row; querying returns it
Second upsert with same (repo_path, pr_number) but updated merged_at replaces the row (still 1 row)
upsert with empty reviews vec sets first_review_at = NULL, iteration_count = 0
upsert with 2 CHANGES_REQUESTED reviews sets iteration_count = 2

userStories.title: Integration test: query_pr_cycle_stats()
userStories.description: In tests/pr_cycle_test.rs, insert rows into pr_snapshots directly via SQL, call query_pr_cycle_stats, assert computed metrics.
acceptanceCriteria: 1 merged PR with 10h cycle time and 2h to first review: median_cycle_time_hours=Some(10.0), median_time_to_first_review_hours=Some(2.0)
2 merged PRs with cycle times 4h and 8h: median = 6.0
1 open PR (no merged_at): cycle_time_hours=None, total_prs=1, merged_prs=0
Date filter: PR outside [from,to] excluded
repo_path_filter: Some('repo-a') excludes repo-b PRs
Empty table: all medians None, counts 0

userStories.title: Integration test: CLI blackbox prs
userStories.description: In tests/pr_cycle_test.rs or tests/cli_test.rs, use assert_cmd to verify blackbox prs runs correctly.
acceptanceCriteria: blackbox prs exits 0 with a populated DB (may show 'No PR data' if no snapshots, but must not panic)
blackbox prs --format json exits 0, stdout parses as valid JSON
blackbox prs --days 0 exits non-zero with message containing '--days must be >= 1'

userStories.title: Remove live enrich_with_prs() call from query path
userStories.description: The existing enrich_with_prs() in main.rs/run_query and standup dispatch fetches live PR data at query time. Now that PR data is persisted via poll, the RepoSummary.pr_info field should be populated from DB instead. Remove or replace the live call.
acceptanceCriteria: enrich_with_prs() calls removed from run_query() in main.rs and from the Standup dispatch block

RepoSummary.pr_info still populated — but now sourced from pr_snapshots via a new query helper, OR field set to None (if PR info display is being deprecated from today/week/month output in favor of the dedicated 'prs' command)
Acceptable option: leave pr_info=None in today/week/month output and document that 'blackbox prs' is the canonical PR view
cargo build passes, existing tests pass
No live gh subprocess calls during 'blackbox today/week/month'

userStories.title: Edge case: closed-without-merge PRs
userStories.description: Ensure closed PRs with no merged_at are stored and handled correctly. cycle_time_hours should be None for these. Display should label them as 'closed' not 'abandoned'.
acceptanceCriteria: PR with state='CLOSED' and mergedAt=null stored with merged_at=NULL in DB
query_pr_cycle_stats returns cycle_time_hours=None for closed-without-merge
render_pr_cycle_stats shows state='closed' (not 'abandoned' or 'rejected')
closed PRs counted in total_prs but not merged_prs
Integration test: insert 1 merged + 1 closed PR, assert merged_prs=1, total_prs=2

userStories.title: Edge case: repos with no PRs / gh not installed
userStories.description: Verify graceful degradation when gh unavailable, repo has no PRs, or API returns empty list.
acceptanceCriteria: collect_pr_snapshots returns immediately if !gh_available() — no log::warn, only debug
fetch_pr_details returning None (e.g. not a GitHub repo) causes that repo to be skipped silently
fetch_pr_details returning Some([]) (empty list) is handled: no upserts, no error
blackbox prs with empty pr_snapshots table shows 'No PR data available for this period' and exits 0
No panics in any of the above cases — all verified by unit or integration tests

userStories.title: Edge case: gh rate limit and large repos
userStories.description: fetch_pr_details uses --limit 50 to cap requests. Document the limit. Ensure rate limit errors (non-zero exit from gh) are handled as None return, not panic.
acceptanceCriteria: --limit 50 hardcoded in fetch_pr_details gh call
Non-zero exit from gh subprocess → return None (already covered by existing pattern but must be verified in test)
Comment in fetch_pr_details documenting: 'limit 50 caps API requests; PRs older than 50 most-recent open/closed may not be captured'
No retry logic — gh manages its own rate limiting

userStories.title: Update README.md and AGENTS.md
userStories.description: Update project documentation to reflect new feature. README.md: add new command to Commands table if applicable, update feature descriptions. AGENTS.md: add new source files to Architecture tree, update Key Patterns if new patterns introduced.
acceptanceCriteria: README.md Commands table includes new command (if a CLI command was added)
README.md describes the new feature/capability
AGENTS.md Architecture src/ tree lists any new .rs files
AGENTS.md Key Dependencies updated if new crates added
userStories.notes: This must be the LAST story executed. Reference README.md and AGENTS.md in project root.
